content.full_text:
California 
Fair Political 
Practices Commission 
June 19, 1989 
Lance H. Olson 
Olson, Connelly, Hagel and Fong 
300 Capitol Mall, suite 350 
Sacramento CA 
95814 
Dear Mr. Olson: 
Re: 
Your Request for Advice 
Our File No. A-89-304 
This is in response to your request for advice regarding the 
duties and responsibilities of the Sacramentans For Hope committee 
under the provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act,,).l 
QUESTION 
Do the name and the statement of organization of the commit-
tee Sacramentans For Hope comply with the requirements of the Act? 
CONCLUSION 
Absent information which rebuts the presumption that Supervi-
sor James Streng controls the actions or decisions of the commit-
tee, the name and the statement of organization of the committee 
Sacramentans For Hope do not comply with the requirements of the 
Act and should be amended. 
FACTS 
Your office represents the Sacramentans For Hope recipient 
committee. 
The committee is registered with the Secretary of 
State as a "primarily formed" committee for the purpose of oppos-
ing Measure L, which appeared on the June 6, 1989, ballot in 
Sacramento County. 
You are seeking advice relative to the duties 
and responsibilities of the committee under the Act. 
Government Code Sections 81000-91015. 
All statutory references 
are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated. 
Commission 
regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations section 
18000, et seq. 
All references to regulations are to Title 2, 
Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations. 
428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804~0807 • 
(916)322~5660 


File No. A-89-304 
Page 2 
The committee is governed by a sixteen-member steering com-
mittee which sets the policies for receipt and expenditure of 
campaign funds. 
Thirteen of the steering committee members serve 
in a representative capacity on behalf of a variety of nonprofit 
organizations in the medical research, health, and academic 
fields. 
Two of the members of the steering committee serve in an 
individual capacity. 
The remaining member of the steering com-
mittee is Sacramento county Supervisor James Streng. 
Many, 
although not all, of the organizations represented on the steering 
committee will be making direct or in-kind contributions to the 
committee. 
The statement of organization on file with the office of the 
Secretary of State lists the name of the committee as Sacramentans 
For Hope -
No on Measure L Sponsored by a Coalition of Academic, 
Health and Research organizations. 
The statement of organization 
indicates that the committee is not controlled by a candidate, of-
ficeholder or state measure proponent and is not acting jointly 
with any other committees. 
The statement of organization does 
indicate that the committee is a sponsored committee and each of 
the thirteen organizations represented on the steering committee 
is listed as a sponsor of the committee. 
The statement of 
organization also indicates that the committee has been primarily 
formed to oppose Measure L on the Sacramento County ballot. 
ANALYSIS 
The Act defines a committee as any person or combination of 
persons who directly or indirectly receives contributions totaling 
one thousand dollars ($1,000) or more in a calendar year. 
(Sec-
tion 82013(a).) 
Committees are required to file a statement of 
organization with the Secretary of State. 
(Section 84101(a).) 
Additionally, Government Code section 82016 defines a 
"controlled committee" as: 
... a committee which is controlled directly or 
indirectly by a candidate or state measure 
proponent or which acts jointly with a candidate, 
controlled committee or state measure proponent in 
connection with the making of expenditures. 
A 
candidate or state measure proponent controls a 
committee if he, his agent or any other committee 
he controls has a significant influence on the ac-
tions or decisions of the committee. 
(Emphasis 
added. ) 
You have stated in your request for advice that Sacramentans 
For Hope is a committee sponsored by a variety of organizations in 
the fields of medical research, health, and academics. 
Addition-
ally, the sixteen member steering committee which sets the 
policies for receipt and expenditure of campaign funds includes 


File No. A-89-304 
Page 3 
Sacramento County su~ervisor James Streng who is a candidate for 
purposes of the Act. 
Section 82048.7 (previously numbered 82048.5) specifically 
excludes controlled committees from the definition of sponsored 
committee. 
This statute provides in pertinent part that: 
"Sponsored committee" means a committee, other 
than a candidate controlled committee, which has 
one or more sponsors. 
Any person, except a 
candidate or other individual, may sponsor a com-
mittee. 
section 82048.7(a). 
Thus, if we determine that Sacramentans For Hope is a controlled 
committee, we cannot also conclude it is a sponsored committee. 
(Scully Advice Letter, No. A-88-054, copy enclosed.) 
A candidate or an elected officeholder who is a voting member 
of a committee's steering committee is presumed to be a "control-
ling candidate" because he probably exercises "significant influ-
ence on the actions or decisions of the committee." 
(Ferguson 
Advice Letter, No. A-86-044, copy enclosed.) 
The Commission has 
interpreted the definition of "controlled committee" very broadly 
to include any significant participation in the actions of a com-
mittee by a candidate, his or her agent, or representatives of any 
other committee he or she controls. 
(Forcier Advice Letter, No. 
I-89-058, copy enclosed.) 
Therefore, absent information that 
demonstrates that Supervisor Streng does not exercise "significant 
influence on the actions or decisions of the committee," 
Sacramentans For Hope is a controlled committee for purposes of 
the Act. 
The contents of the statement of organization of a controlled 
committee must comply with specific requirements of the Act. 
Sec-
tion 84106, added to the Act by Proposition 68, has been 
determined to be not in conflict with Proposition 73 and severable 
from the other provisions of Proposition 68. 
(In re Bell (1988) 
11 FPPC Ops. 1, copy enclosed.) 
It requires that the name of a 
committee include or be accompanied by the name of any individual, 
entity or other person controlling the committee. 
(See also 
Bagatelos Advice Letter, No. I-88-475, copy enclosed.) 
The committee Sacramentans For Hope includes an elected of-
ficial in its steering committee. 
It is, therefore, a controlled 
committee and the statement of organization should be amended to 
include the name of Sacramento County supervisor James streng in 
2 
An elected official is a candidate for purposes of 
individual who becomes a candidate retains his status 
candidate until that status is officially terminated. 
Code Section 82007.) 
the Act. 
An 
as a 
(Government 


File No. A-89-304 
Page 4 
the name of the committee. 
In the alternative, Supervisor 
Streng's name must accompany the name of the committee. 
Additionally, if the committee supports or opposes a ballot 
measure as its primary activity, the name of the committee also 
must identify the measure's ballot number or letter and indicate 
whether the committee supports or opposes the measure. 
(Section 
84107. ) 
The amended statement of organization need not include the 
names of those sponsoring the committee because a controlled com-
mittee need not meet the sponsored committee requirements of the 
Act. 
We have determined that Sacramentans For Hope is a 
controlled committee. 
Thus, the amended statement of organization 
need only address the requirements of section 84106, as added by 
Proposition 68. 
A candidate controlled committee is not required 
to meet the sponsored committee requirements of the Act. 
(Section 
84028.7; Bagatelos Advice Letter, supra.) 
Should you have any further questions regarding the above 
matter, do not hesitate to call me at (916) 322-5901. 
KED:BMB:plh 
Enclosures 
Sincerely, 
Kathryn E. Donovan 
General Counsel

Sacramento, California

RE: 
SACRAMENTANS FOR HOPE -
MEASURE L 
Dear Ms. Donovan: 
This office represents the above-referenced 
Recipient Committee. 
The Committee is registered with 
the Secretary of State as a "primarily formed il 
Committee for the purpose of opposing Measure L which 
will appear on the June 6, 1989 ballot in Sacramento 
County. 
I am seeking advice relative to the 
Committee's duties and responsibilities under the 
Political Reform Act of 1974 as amended. 
The Committee is governed by a sixteen member 
steering committee which sets the policies for receipt 
and expenditure of campaign funds. 
Thirteen of the 
steering committee members serve in a representative 
capacity on behalf of a variety of non-profit 
organizations in the fields of medical research, health 
and academics. 
Two of the members of the steering 
committee serve in an individual capacity. 
The final 
member of the steering committee is Sacramento County 
Supervisor James Streng. 
Many, although not all, of 
the organizations represented on the steering committee 
will be making direct or in-kind contributions to the 
LANCE H. OLSON 
commi ttee. 
BRUCE J. HAGEL 
LEROY Y. FONG 
The statement of Organization on file with the 
ROBERT E. LEIDIGH 
Secretary of state's office lists the name of the 
Committee as follows, Sacramentans for Hope -
No on 
Measure L Sponsored by a Coalition of Academic, Health 
and Research Organizations. 
The Statement of 
Organization indicates that the Committee is not 
OF COUNSEL 
controlled by a candidate office-holder or State 
LLOYD G. CONNELLY, Member measure proponent and is not acting jointly with any 
California State Legislature 
other committees. 
The Statement of Organization does 
indicate that the Committee is a sponsored Committee 
and each of the thirteen organizations represented on 
the steering committee are listed as sponsors of the 
Committee. 
300 
Mall. Suite 350 
Sacramento, California 95814 
I:LI:l'TlU,'" (916) 442-2952 
FAX (916) 442-1280 


May 19, 1989 
Page Two 
Finally the statement of Organization indicates that the 
Committee's primarily formed to oppose Measure L on the 
Sacramento County ballot. 
The Committee wishes to know whether its name and the 
Statement of Organization complies with the provisions of the 
Political Reform Act. 
I would appreciate your prompt response in view of the fact 
that Measure L will be voted upon by the voters relatively soon. 
Very truly yours, 
OLSON, CONNELLY, HAGEL & FONG 
LANCE H. OLSON

Re: 
Letter No. 89-304 
Dear Mr. Olson: 
Your letter requesting advice under the Political Reform Act 
was received on May 19, 1989 by the Fair Political Practices 
Commission. 
If you have any questions about your advice request, 
you may contact Margaret Ellison an attorney in the Legal 
Division, directly at (916) 322-5901. 
We try to answer all advice requests promptly. 
Therefore, 
unless your request poses particularly complex legal questions, or 
more information is needed, you should expect a response within 21 
working days if your request seeks formal written advice. 
If more 
information is needed, the person assigned to prepare a response 
to your request will contact you shortly to advise you as to 
information needed. 
If your request is for informal assistance, 
we will answer it as quickly as we can. 
(See Commission 
Regulation 18329 (2 Cal. Code of Regs. Sec. 18329).) 
You also should be aware that your letter and our response 
are public records which may be disclosed to the public upon 
receipt of a proper request for disclosure.

tion 82013(a).)

organization with the Secretary of State.

proponent or which acts jointly with a candidate, 
controlled committee or state measure proponent in 
connection with the making of expenditures. 
A 
candidate or state measure proponent controls a 
committee if he, his agent or any other committee 
he controls has a significant influence on the ac-
tions or decisions of the committee. 
(Emphasis 
added. ) 
You have stated in your request for advice that Sacramentans 
For Hope is a committee sponsored by a variety of organizations in 
the fields of medical research, health, and academics.

policies for receipt and expenditure of campaign funds includes

purposes of the Act.

"Sponsored committee" means a committee, other 
than a candidate controlled committee, which has 
one or more sponsors. 
Any person, except a 
candidate or other individual, may sponsor a com-
mittee.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Do the name and the statement of organization of the commit-
tee Sacramentans For Hope comply with the requirements of the Act?

CONCLUSION: Absent information which rebuts the presumption that Supervi-
sor James Streng controls the actions or decisions of the commit-
tee, the name and the statement of organization of the committee 
Sacramentans For Hope do not comply with the requirements of the 
Act and should be amended.

Olson Olson, Connelly, Hagel and Fong 300 Capitol Mall, suite 350 Sacramento CA 95814 Dear Mr. Olson: Re: Your Request for Advice Our File No. A-89-304 This is in response to your request for advice regarding the duties and responsibilities of the Sacramentans For Hope committee under the provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act,,).l QUESTION Do the name and the statement of organization of the commit- tee Sacramentans For Hope comply with the requirements of the Act? CONCLUSION Absent information which rebuts the presumption that Supervi- sor James Streng controls the actions or decisions of the commit- tee, the name and the statement of organization of the committee Sacramentans For Hope do not comply with the requirements of the Act and should be amended. FACTS Your office represents the Sacramentans For Hope recipient committee. The committee is registered with the Secretary of State as a "primarily formed" committee for the purpose of oppos- ing Measure L, which appeared on the June 6, 1989, ballot in Sacramento County. You are seeking advice relative to the duties and responsibilities of the committee under the Act. Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory references are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated. Commission regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations section 18000, et seq. All references to regulations are to Title 2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations. 428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804~0807 • (916)322~5660 File No. A-89-304 Page 2 The committee is governed by a sixteen-member steering com- mittee which sets the policies for receipt and expenditure of campaign funds. Thirteen of the steering committee members serve in a representative capacity on behalf of a variety of nonprofit organizations in the medical research, health, and academic fields. Two of the members of the steering committee serve in an individual capacity. The remaining member of the steering com- mittee is Sacramento county Supervisor James Streng. Many, although not all, of the organizations represented on the steering committee will be making direct or in-kind contributions to the committee. The statement of organization on file with the office of the Secretary of State lists the name of the committee as Sacramentans For Hope - No on Measure L Sponsored by a Coalition of Academic, Health and Research organizations. The statement of organization indicates that the committee is not controlled by a candidate, of- ficeholder or state measure proponent and is not acting jointly with any other committees. The statement of organization does indicate that the committee is a sponsored committee and each of the thirteen organizations represented on the steering committee is listed as a sponsor of the committee. The statement of organization also indicates that the committee has been primarily formed to oppose Measure L on the Sacramento County ballot. ANALYSIS The Act defines a committee as any person or combination of persons who directly or indirectly receives contributions totaling one thousand
